frames.name: Administrative Confusion Frame
frames.description: Focuses on contradictory explanations from Trump administration officials about justifications for striking Iran, portraying policy confusion and lack of clear messaging
frames.emphasis: Multiple, shifting rationales from different officials (missile threats, nuclear prevention, preemptive action) and Trump's evolving explanations

frames.name: Strategic Cost-Benefit Analysis Frame
frames.description: Presents the Iran strikes as a complex strategic decision with clear pros and cons, analyzing whether military action makes Americans safer or more at risk
frames.emphasis: Systematic evaluation of military benefits (degraded capabilities, weakened proxy network, maintained credibility) versus risks (retaliation, escalation)

frames.name: Breaking News/Crisis Frame
frames.description: Emphasizes immediate developments, escalation, and real-time impacts including casualties, market reactions, and regional spread
frames.emphasis: 11 countries involved, 6 US service members killed, market sell-offs, embassy closures, civilian impact in Beirut

outlierPerspectives: Trump's claim that he 'might have forced Israel's hand' rather than vice versa - a reversal of typical narratives about Israeli influence on US policy
Vox's systematic framework analyzing Iran strikes through lens of whether they make Americans safer or less safe
Focus on Iran's surprise targeting of 'neutral countries' as mentioned by Trump
missingContext: Timeline of events leading to the strikes - what specific incidents or intelligence triggered the action
Congressional authorization or legal justification for military action against Iran
International allies' positions and reactions to US-Israeli strikes
Specific details about the 'negotiations with these lunatics' Trump referenced
Casualty figures and humanitarian impact in Iran from the strikes
Previous US-Iran tensions and escalatory cycles for historical context
